jobs.title: Head/Director of Product (AI Insights & Automation)
jobs.recommendation: Apply — right level (Head/Director), strong AI product fit (enterprise SaaS automation), EU remote, scale-up. GelatoConnect AI Estimator directly parallels Mimica's process automation product. Posted 12 hours ago.
jobs.notes: London-based AI process intelligence startup. Role is fully remote EU/NA timezone. Requires: Head/Director/VP level scale-up experience, AI/ML product experience, enterprise SaaS. Pradeep's AI Estimator ($0→$2M ARR, 30-60min→15sec) is a direct parallel. Also posted as job 4432441165 (Ireland Remote listing).
jobs.cover_letter:
Most process intelligence tools show you what's happening but leave you to decide what to do about it. The more interesting version of this category — the one Mimica is building — closes that gap: learn from human behavior, surface the insight, and then automate the repetition. That's a fundamentally harder product problem, and it's the right one to be solving.

At Gelato, I faced a version of this exact challenge: a complex, expert-dependent pricing workflow that took 30–60 minutes per job and blocked growth. I rebuilt it as the GelatoConnect AI Estimator — deploying a model that captured the logic embedded in expert decisions and delivered quotes in 15 seconds. $0 to $2M ARR in six months, 65% reduction in manual effort. The AI didn't replace judgment; it extracted and operationalized it.

I'm curious how Mimica thinks about the boundary between assisted automation and fully autonomous execution — that's where the product roadmap gets interesting.

Happy to connect.

archive.title: Product Director – Core Marketplace Platforms
archive.notes: Vend ($4.97B market cap) owns FINN.no, Blocket, Tori and other Nordic marketplaces. 5 verticals (Mobility, Real Estate, Jobs, Recommerce, Delivery). Director-level, Oslo. Platform/marketplace architecture challenge. Listed Sep 2025 but still active. Norwegian language not required (English-language posting).
archive.cover_letter:
Vend runs five verticals across the Nordic marketplace estate, and the core platform underpinning FINN, Blocket, Tori and the rest carries a specific and difficult tension: build for the shared need without slow-walking the verticals that have their own growth trajectories. Getting that balance wrong either produces an over-engineered core that nobody wants to depend on, or a fragmented estate where every vertical reinvents the same wheel.

At Gelato, I led product for a global platform operating across 100+ countries and multiple product verticals - print, apparel, wall art, packaging. The core-versus-vertical split was a constant architectural and organisational challenge. A concrete result: I shaped the platform architecture that let us launch the AI Estimator as a shared capability, going from zero to $2M ARR in six months because it served multiple verticals without requiring separate product builds for each.

Recommerce and Delivery look like the emerging bets where strong platform investment gives Vend a compounding structural advantage. The Product Director role seems designed to make that happen - and building shared capabilities that accelerate rather than constrain vertical teams is exactly what I have spent the last several years doing at scale.

Happy to connect.

archive.title: Head of Product - Platform
archive.recommendation: Apply — B2B SaaS AI platform role (AML/fraud detection), platform product leadership, remote-Europe friendly. Directly maps to Gelato platform experience.
archive.notes: AI-powered AML & fraud detection for banks. Platform role owns infra/scalability across 3 product divisions, SaaS + private cloud deployment models, enterprise pricing. London or Munich hybrid. Posted via Ashby: bcb2d04d-9ebd-4c7c-b2b5-a53a00d375e5
archive.cover_letter:
Financial crime detection is moving from deterministic rules to explainable AI — but for banks and payment providers operating under constant regulatory scrutiny, the product challenge isn't just building better models. It's making those models deployable, auditable, and trustworthy at enterprise scale. That's not a data science problem. That's a platform problem — and it's where Head of Product Platform at Hawk sits.

At Gelato, I owned the platform layer connecting 130+ production partners across 100+ countries — the infrastructure layer that enterprise customers and internal teams depended on to build everything else. The challenge maps directly: enterprise deployments that had to work across SaaS, managed, and API-first configurations, with reliability guarantees that ops teams could stake their workflows on. The GelatoConnect platform we built took quote time from 30–60 minutes to 15 seconds — not by adding AI on top, but by rethinking the platform data model underneath.

How is Hawk thinking about explainability and model transparency as a first-class platform capability, rather than a compliance afterthought? That is the kind of hard architectural question I find most interesting. Happy to connect.

archive.title: Head of Product - FCP - Tietoevry Banking
archive.notes: Target company. Different BU from existing Public 360 application (ID 6). Banking FCP (Financial Crime Prevention - identity/fraud/AML) is a Nordic market leader. Reports to Head of FCP at Tietoevry Banking. Fornebu is Oslo area. Strong platform + AI + Nordic fit. Complementary to Public 360 track.
archive.cover_letter:
Financial crime prevention is being reshaped by AI, but for banking platforms serving Nordic tier-1 institutions the real product challenge isn't the models themselves. It's making them explainable, auditable, and deployable inside the operational workflows of fraud and AML teams who carry regulatory accountability. That is a platform problem, not a data science problem, and it's where Head of Product, FCP sits.

At Gelato I led product for a global B2B platform connecting 130+ production partners across 100+ countries. The GelatoConnect AI Estimator went from zero to $2M ARR in six months by rethinking how platform data models surfaced decisions inside customer workflows, not by bolting AI on top. That same discipline, AI as a first-class platform capability rather than a feature, maps directly to what FCP needs as Nordic banks face rising scrutiny on model explainability and adverse-action auditability.

How is Tietoevry thinking about bringing compliance officers into the model feedback loop as a product surface, rather than an operational afterthought?

Happy to connect.

archive.notes: Bulgarian telecom/connectivity going global via hybrid Starlink+fiber+5G. Fundamental transformation moment, CPO reports to exec team. Strong domain parallel to Gelato's global platform play. B2B enterprise focus. | [Re-saved 2026-05-17 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-05-20 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-05-26 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-05-27 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-07 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-08 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-09 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-10 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-13 — previously Applied; reconsider?]
archive.cover_letter:
Neterra's transformation — operating globally through a hybrid of Starlink, fiber and 5G — is a specific kind of hard. You are asking enterprise customers to buy into a single product experience that masks enormous infrastructure complexity underneath, across suppliers and geographies. That is the same transformation I have spent the last four years leading at Gelato.

Gelato built the operating system for a $1T global print industry — a platform that spans 100+ countries, connects thousands of production partners, and delivers a single API surface to enterprise buyers. I led the product org through this. Most recently I shipped GelatoConnect AI Estimator: $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months, 65% reduction in manual quoting, quote time from 30–60 minutes to 15 seconds. Earlier: 11 years at Schlumberger across 15 countries, building the kind of high-reliability industrial systems telecom networks depend on. Both sides of this experience map directly to Neterra's inflection point.

What does Neterra's product look like when the buying decision moves from "which network provider" to "which global connectivity platform"? Happy to connect.

archive.title: VP of Product Management
archive.recommendation: Apply — VP level, agentic AI + industrial IoT data platform, EU remote. Compensation $220K-$330K. Reports to CPO. Aligns with Gelato AI Estimator and Schlumberger backgrounds.
archive.notes: Posted 14 hours ago, 200+ applicants. Product portfolio: data streaming, data intelligence, agentic AI. Works in tight Product-Design-Engineering triangle with CTO and VP Engineering.
archive.cover_letter:
HiveMQ's shift from MQTT broker to owning data streaming, data intelligence, and agentic AI in one platform is one of the more interesting product bets in industrial infrastructure right now — you control the data ingest layer, which means you can build AI workflows without depending on a third-party data layer that may not reflect what's actually happening on the factory floor.

At Gelato, I built the AI Estimator from $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months — an agentic workflow applied at a high-friction industrial handoff point that collapsed quote time from 30–60 minutes to 15 seconds. That is exactly the product pattern HiveMQ is now scaling across data intelligence and agentic AI. Eleven years at Schlumberger across 15 countries also gave me a clear read on how industrial operators actually adopt new software: reliability first, self-serve much later.

The question I keep coming back to: does the agentic AI layer expand HiveMQ's market by converting industrial ops teams who never bought messaging infrastructure, or does it reshape the pricing model as customers want pre-built pipelines rather than raw broker capacity? That tension seems like the defining strategic question for the VP Product right now. Happy to connect.

archive.title: Director of Product Management
archive.notes: Lead Design Apps portfolio across Forma platform (Site/Building/Street/Land Design + Design Automations); leads 4-5 PMs. Director not VP, but Oslo + global software giant + AEC SaaS scope = strong on level-flex rule. Domain (AEC) is adjacent to Pradeep's industrial SaaS background. Posted ~21 hours ago. | [Re-saved 2026-05-16 - previously Will not apply; reconsider?]
archive.cover_letter:
Forma sits at an unusual inflection point — Autodesk has the dominant install base in AEC, but the move from desktop CAD to cloud-native, AI-assisted design is a different kind of product motion entirely. Holding strategy across Site, Building, Street, Land, and Design Automations as that maturity curve plays out is exactly the kind of high-leverage, multi-product portfolio role that I find interesting.

At Gelato I built and shipped GelatoConnect's AI Estimator from $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months — cutting quote time from 30-60 minutes to 15 seconds and removing 65% of the manual work in the print production sales loop. Before Gelato, eleven years at Schlumberger across fifteen countries gave me deep instincts for how technical professionals actually adopt new software in the field — the kind of intuition that matters when the buyer is an architect or engineer, not a digital-native PM.

What does Forma look like in five years if generative AI absorbs 30-40% of the early-stage design work? Happy to connect.

archive.title: VP Product - Custom Engineering & Integrations
archive.notes: GenAI adtech SaaS. VP Product role for Custom Engineering & Integrations vertical — owns model for enterprise integrations & custom workflows. Remote EMEA, work from home country. 200+ applicants — competitive but level-matched. Strong domain rhyme with GelatoConnect AI Estimator (genAI product + enterprise integrations). User-saved on LinkedIn. | [Re-saved 2026-05-16 - previously Will not apply; reconsider?]
archive.cover_letter:
Pencil sits at an interesting hinge in adtech — generative AI made content creation a commodity, so the moat shifts to integrations: the depth of data, workflow, and platform connections that let a brand actually deploy AI-generated work at scale. A VP Product role anchored on Custom Engineering & Integrations is exactly that bet, and it is a genuinely hard product problem because the customer roadmap fragments by ecosystem.

I built and shipped GelatoConnect's AI Estimator at Gelato — a generative-AI product that went from $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months by collapsing a 30-60 minute manual quoting workflow into 15 seconds, removing 65% of the manual effort. The platform integrates into print producers' existing MIS, ERP, and shipping systems, so I have lived the same integrations-as-product motion you are scaling. Eleven years at Schlumberger before that gave me deep instincts for selling complex software into enterprise buyers with their own technology stacks.

How does Pencil think about the trade-off between deepening the core platform and shipping bespoke integration work as your enterprise pipeline matures? Happy to connect.

archive.title: CTO/CPO
archive.notes: ⚠️ Non-Oslo hybrid — included because user-saved on LinkedIn AND Depict is YC/EQT Ventures-backed Nordic-ecosystem AI retail intelligence (Stockholm = ~1hr flight from Oslo). Verify openness to Oslo-based hire before applying. Combined CTO+CPO role typical of early-stage — fit on product strong, on engineering management is a stretch. Backed by Tiger Global, Initialized, EQT Ventures, YC. 127 applicants. | [Re-saved 2026-05-16 - previously Will not apply; reconsider?]
archive.cover_letter:
Retail merchandising has been the same workflow for thirty years — humans dragging products into rows on category pages. Depict's bet that applied AI fundamentally restructures that motion, not just speeds it up, is the right kind of hard. Combining the CTO and CPO role into one says you want a hire who can hold the technical architecture and the product narrative as one decision, which is the only way that bet actually ships.

At Gelato I built the AI Estimator from $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months — a generative AI product embedded in a complex production workflow, where the hard part was the data, the integrations, and the trust, not the model. I have spent the last few years operating at SVP Product scale on a global SaaS platform, and before that eleven years at Schlumberger across fifteen countries running technical operations. I am Oslo-based, but Stockholm is a one-hour flight and EQT Ventures' Nordic footprint suggests Depict already thinks across the region.

What is Depict's view on the right shape of the early product/engineering org as you scale past your first cohort of enterprise customers? Happy to connect.

archive.title: Avdelingsdirektør for produktutvikling

archive.title: Chief Product Technology Officer
archive.notes: Norway's central construction-industry digital information provider — pricing data, product catalogs, building documentation. Combined CTO+CPO C-level role. Owns product strategy, portfolio, tech architecture (Azure-based), distributed teams, and AI-assisted development. Oslo on-site. Industrial digital transformation play — strong fit with Pradeep's Schlumberger industrial-ops + Gelato platform/AI background.
archive.cover_letter:
Byggtjeneste sits in an unusual seat — the digital backbone of an entire industry that is still running on documents, PDFs, and tribal knowledge. The product opportunity here is not building features. It is whether construction in Norway will be a queryable, structured, AI-ready supply chain ten years from now, and Byggtjeneste owns the single most defensible position to make that happen.

That kind of foundational, infrastructure-style product role is where I have spent the last several years. As SVP Product at Gelato, I built the AI Estimator from $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months, cutting quote times from 30–60 minutes to 15 seconds and reducing manual quoting effort by 65% — replacing the same kind of expert-knowledge-locked-in-spreadsheets workflow that legacy industries run on. Before Gelato, I spent 11 years at Schlumberger across 15 countries operating high-reliability industrial systems in field environments, so the cultural reality of long-cycle Norwegian B2B is not theoretical for me.

Curious how you are thinking about the platform vs. AI-application boundary as Azure-based foundations mature. Happy to connect.

Pradeep

archive.title: Chief Product Officer (Europe)
archive.notes: CPO Europe role for Korea's leading productivity-software company expanding into Europe with an agentic AI operating system. Remote/Europe-based, reports directly to CEO, leads product strategy and POC pipeline. Posted 1 day ago, 115 applicants. Posting also references Drammen, Norway as a location option. | [Re-saved 2026-05-17 — already tracked, no action] | [Re-saved 2026-05-20 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-05-26 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-05-27 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-07 — previously Applied; reconsider?] | [Re-saved 2026-06-08 as 'CPO (Europe)' — previously Applied; reconsider?]
archive.cover_letter: HANCOM's bet on an agentic AI OS for European knowledge workers is interesting because the hard problem is not the agent's capability, it is displacing the daily habits of professionals already locked into Microsoft and Google. That is a product distribution problem more than a research problem, and it lives or dies on POC design, integration choices, and the speed at which early proof points can be turned into a repeatable European motion. Greenfield, CEO-reporting, C-suite mandate. That is the kind of role I would build a career chapter around. At Gelato I led the product launch that took the GelatoConnect AI Estimator from zero to $2M+ ARR in six months, cutting industrial quote workflows from 30 to 60 minutes down to 15 seconds. The model was the easy part. Rebuilding the surrounding workflow so the AI output got trusted, integrated, and adopted across 100+ countries was the real product work. Before Gelato, eleven years at Schlumberger across 15 countries shipping high-reliability industrial systems. I am curious how HANCOM is thinking about data residency and the European workflow surface as Microsoft and Google harden their own agentic plays. Happy to connect.

archive.title: Vice President of Product Management
archive.notes: VP Product reporting to CEO. Retail analytics platform integrating AI with deep retail expertise. Owns the in-store sensor data layer (physical-store analytics) — differentiated against online-clickstream competitors. EEA Remote — clean fit on level + remote-EU geography + AI/data domain. Posted 2 days ago. | [Re-saved 2026-06-06 — already tracked, no action]
archive.cover_letter:
RetailNext owns the in-store data layer that almost nobody else has. Most retail analytics startups compete for the same online clickstream; you sit on the physical-store sensor signal. The interesting product question right now is not whether the data is good. It is what the retail analyst's job looks like when AI is doing the analysis and the product needs to surface a decision rather than a dashboard.

At Gelato, where I am SVP Product, that is exactly the shift we have been making on a global manufacturing platform spanning 100+ countries. The clearest example is the GelatoConnect AI Estimator I built: zero to $2M+ ARR in six months, 65% reduction in manual quoting effort, quote time cut from 30 to 60 minutes down to 15 seconds. The hard part was not the model. The hard part was reshaping the product so the AI's answer was trustable, traceable, and overridable by the human operator. Before Gelato I spent 11 years at Schlumberger across 15 countries, which is where I learned how to ship to high-reliability operational users who do not tolerate AI hand-waving.

What does RetailNext's product surface look like in a world where the retail merchandiser asks a question and gets an answer, not a report? Happy to connect.

archive.title: Director of Product Management
archive.recommendation: Apply this week. Autodesk Forma Design Apps portfolio (Site Design, Building Design, Street Design, Land Development) leading 4-5 PMs. Cloud-native apps inside desktop-native co; shared data platform (Scenarios). Director-level, Oslo on-site, posted 3 days ago, 62 applicants already - move fast.
archive.notes: Forma = AEC industry (architecture/engineering/construction) CAD platform. Portfolio + platform pattern matches Gelato/GelatoConnect experience. Big co (Autodesk public ~$6B revenue) but Forma is a newer cloud-native bet. Onsite Oslo is perfect location match.
archive.cover_letter:
Autodesk's Forma Design Apps sit at a tension few B2B teams face. You are building cloud-native apps inside a desktop-native company, on a shared data platform (Scenarios) where every product decision ripples across four maturity stages. Most candidates will treat that as a footnote. It is actually the entire job.

At Gelato I spent the last few years on exactly this shape, a global platform product (GelatoConnect) where the data layer is the moat, and each application sits at a different maturity. We took the AI Estimator from zero to $2M+ ARR in six months and cut manual quoting time from 30 minutes to 15 seconds. The harder work was deciding which application bets deserved platform investment first, and which to ship on top of what existed. Before Gelato, eleven years at Schlumberger across fifteen countries taught me to make sophisticated industrial software adoptable by non-software users.

The Scenarios platform feels like the long-term lever, every Forma app gets sharper as the substrate grows. How does the team weigh which application defines the next layer of Scenarios capability versus consuming what is there?

Happy to connect.

archive.title: Director, Product Building
archive.notes: Leads new 25-person 'Experience' sub-division implementing 'Builder' operating model (unified product/design/eng). Shipping AI-native builder experience and revised Camunda Hub. Workflow automation category facing AI-agent disruption — strategic inflection. Remote EU acceptable; Camunda is Berlin-HQ distributed. Posted 2 days ago.
archive.cover_letter:
Workflow automation is the category most directly tested by AI agents, because the abstraction Camunda has sold for years, orchestrating humans and systems via BPMN, suddenly competes with the prospect of a model orchestrating the work itself. The bet I read into the Builder operating model and the AI-native builder experience is that AI accelerates the human authoring loop rather than replacing it. That is the right bet, and it is also the one with the hardest product execution problem behind it.

I have run exactly that pattern at scale. As SVP Product at Gelato I shipped the GelatoConnect AI Estimator, taking a 30 to 60 minute human quoting workflow down to 15 seconds, while keeping the human in the loop for the edge cases the model could not be trusted on. Zero to $2M+ ARR in six months, 65% reduction in manual quoting effort. Before that, 11 years at Schlumberger across 15 countries shipping high-reliability software to industrial-ops users. The unification of product, design, and engineering you are aiming for with the Builder sub-division is also how I have run my squads at Gelato.

How does Camunda think about the line between AI generates the workflow and the human authors with AI assistance, and where do you expect that line to sit in 18 months? Happy to connect.

archive.title: Head of Product
archive.recommendation: US-Remote location test — German parent (SAP), global supply chain finance, no explicit US work-auth language. SAP has Norway office so Oslo-hire is plausible. Test: will they entertain Oslo-based candidate?
archive.notes: [US-Remote test 2026-06-07] SAP Taulia = SAP subsidiary, 2M+ businesses, 150+ countries, supply chain finance (Payables, Receivables, Virtual Cards). No 'must be authorized to work in US' language in JD. SAP Norway entity exists = could employ Oslo-based. Domain gap: requires fintech/trade finance + SAP ecosystem expertise. Apply to test location openness.
archive.cover_letter:
Trade finance has a version of the "boring infrastructure" problem that makes it more interesting than it looks. Taulia processes real cash flow between some of the world's largest buyers and their supplier networks — the moment a payable moves is actually a decision point, and the question is how much intelligence you can embed at that moment. Most working capital platforms optimise the transaction; Taulia has the position to optimise the relationship.

At Gelato, I built a global manufacturing platform connecting buyers and suppliers at scale across 130+ production partners in 34 countries — which sounds different but shares the core challenge: designing product that works at the intersection of financial flows, operational constraints, and multi-party trust. The GelatoConnect AI Estimator went from $0 to $2M+ ARR in 6 months by embedding intelligence at exactly the right friction point in the quoting workflow. Before product, eleven years at Schlumberger running operations across 15 countries gave me a ground-level education in how multi-national supply chains actually behave when regulatory environments shift.

How are you thinking about the role of AI in early payment decisions — specifically whether the system should recommend or decide, and who owns that liability conversation with large enterprise buyers? Happy to connect.

archive.title: VP, Product Management - Core Platform Foundations
archive.recommendation: US-Remote location test — 6000-person global enterprise CX/AI SaaS, EU offices (Dublin/London/Amsterdam), no explicit US work-auth language. Core Platform role parallels GelatoConnect API/platform work. Test: will they entertain Oslo-based candidate?
archive.notes: [US-Remote test 2026-06-07] Genesys Cloud = AI-powered CX platform, 8000+ enterprise customers globally. EU offices confirmed. No 'authorized to work in US' language in JD. Role owns access control, identity, admin, automation primitives — parallels Gelato platform architecture. Requires 15+ yrs enterprise SaaS + 10+ yrs leading PMs (may be a stretch). Apply to test location openness.
archive.cover_letter:
Platform foundations are the product most people never think about — until something breaks, or until they become the thing that lets every other team move faster than the competition. At Genesys, getting access control, identity, and automation primitives right isn't a back-office problem; it's what determines whether an AI orchestration layer can actually be trusted at enterprise scale.

At Gelato, I owned the GelatoConnect platform end-to-end — an API-first system connecting 14M+ customers to 130+ manufacturing partners globally. The hardest product problems weren't the AI features; they were the foundational ones: how do you design an API surface that scales across hundreds of partner integrations without becoming a maintenance tax? How do you build administration primitives that give enterprise customers control without breaking the automation that makes the platform valuable? GelatoConnect went from a fragmented partner ecosystem to a platform that cut quote time from 30-60 minutes to 15 seconds — and the foundation made that possible.

As you evolve from reactive administration to AI-assisted experiences, how are you thinking about the boundary between what the platform decides autonomously and what it surfaces to admins — especially in regulated industries like healthcare and financial services? Happy to connect.

archive.title: Head of Product (VP Level)
archive.recommendation: Apply — right level (VP-level), right domain (AI-native B2B SaaS), EU-compatible timezone. Verify UK work authorization before applying.
archive.notes: AI-native B2B SaaS at intersection of global employment / fintech / payroll / workforce infrastructure. VP-level role reporting to CEO. Responsibilities: lead product and design teams, drive AI-native transformation, define product vision and roadmap. Salary £120K–£180K. Placed by AJ EXEC executive search firm — actual company name undisclosed. UK remote: verify whether UK residence/work authorization is required before applying.

archive.title: Program Director, Kahoot at Work
archive.notes: [Saved 2026-06-13 — reviewed, won't apply] Wrong function: Program Director is a GTM/commercial/spokesperson role for Kahoot 360 (L&D product), not product management leadership. Responsibilities include industry events, media representation, and narrative shaping — not product strategy or roadmap ownership.

archive.title: Director of Product Management
archive.recommendation: Apply. Oslo on-site, Director of Product Management level, Halliburton Landmark division (energy software). Exceptional domain fit — Pradeep's 11 years at Schlumberger maps directly. Cloud/IT/OT roadmap role for digital solutions. Posted 1 day ago, 25 applicants.
archive.notes: [Saved 2026-06-13] Halliburton Landmark = oil & gas digital/software division. Role covers cloud, infrastructure, IT/OT innovation. Perfect domain fit given Schlumberger background. Active listing confirmed. | [Re-saved 2026-06-15 — already tracked, no action]
archive.cover_letter:
Landmark sits at an inflection I know from the inside. Eleven years at Schlumberger across fifteen countries — drilling, completions, field environments where software failure is not an inconvenience — taught me exactly how energy operators adopt new technology: slowly, with deep skepticism, and only when reliability is proven before capability is added. The move to cloud-native and AI-driven workflows in this industry is real, but the sequencing has to respect that constraint in a way that pure-play SaaS rarely does.

At Gelato I built the other side of that muscle: moving fast, shipping the AI Estimator from $0 to $2M+ ARR in six months, cutting quote time from 30–60 minutes to 15 seconds, and removing 65% of the manual effort from the print production sales loop. Distributed product teams, platform-first architecture, cross-functional alignment at scale — that is the recent chapter. But the Schlumberger years are what make this role specific rather than just senior.

How does Landmark sequence the cloud migration against the AI investment? There is a real tension between rebuilding the infrastructure layer and adding net-new capabilities on top — and getting the order wrong costs years. That is the kind of hard that I find interesting.

Happy to connect.
